title: Onboarding model — /context init = standing incremental registry-population surfaces; accept-all | step-through
context: DEC-0011 (ship no defaults) is violated by initProject auto-copying defaults/ (FGAP-067), and a fresh adopter has no guided path to a conception (FGAP-066). The onboarding interaction shape was unarticulated; the unit-2.2 dialogue (2026-05-20) articulated it.
decision: Onboarding is a conversation that populates the config registries from the samples catalog (DEC-0037), conducted by the harness-confined main LLM via tools (the pi.sendMessage follow-up-turn pattern, DEC-0014-clean; lens-curate is the precedent). /context init is the FIRST PASS of standing, incrementally-invokable registry-population surfaces — the same surfaces later add a block kind, add a relation, or relabel — NOT a one-shot wizard; revisability is therefore structural. Two modes: accept-all (take the platonic bundle wholesale) and step-through (review / relabel / subset one registry entry at a time). The step-through UX is a bounded pi-sdk/TUI investigation, adopting canonical exemplars rather than inventing. Bespoke net-new block-kind authoring (a kind absent from the platonic set) is out of this scope — ship and subset the platonic conception first.
consequences: init = first invocation of permanent surfaces, so add/relabel later reuse the same machinery (no separate wizard).
accept-all is the trivial MVP onboarding; step-through is a separate UX-investigation track.
Depends on the samples discovery surface (FGAP-068) and the per-registry register/relabel write surfaces; the canonical_id-revise leg is the rename engine (FGAP-060).
Supersedes the initProject defaults-imposition (FGAP-067): onboarding offers, never imposes.
Bespoke-kind authoring deferred as a later, separate capability (not silently dropped).

options_considered.label: Standing incremental surfaces, init=first pass (chosen)
options_considered.description: One set of registry-population surfaces; init is just the empty-state entry; add/relabel reuse them.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Requires the surfaces to be CRUD-shaped from day one rather than init-only.

options_considered.label: One-shot init wizard
options_considered.description: A distinct guided wizard that runs once at setup.
options_considered.rejected_reason: Makes revisability a bolt-on; duplicates logic between init and later edits; the user rejected optional/one-shot framing.
